design_philosophy.emotional_archetype: The Electric Street (High Contrast, Trustworthy, Bold)
core_principles: Darkness as Canvas: Use deep obsidian backgrounds to make neon accents pop.
Density & Clarity: Retail is about inventory. Show variety without clutter using bento grids.
Direct Action: 'Call', 'Directions', 'Fix Now' must be high-contrast and immediate.
Authenticity: Prioritize real store photos over stock images where possible.

headings.usage: Hero titles, Section headers, Product categories

grid_system.marketing: Grid cols-1 md:cols-12 with asymmetric spans (Bento Grid)

buttons.primary: bg-[#007AFF] text-white hover:bg-[#0066CC] font-bold uppercase tracking-wide py-3 px-6 shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(0,122,255,0.3)] transition-all
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent border border-[#39FF14] text-[#39FF14] hover:bg-[#39FF14] hover:text-black font-bold uppercase tracking-wide py-3 px-6 transition-all

cards.service_card: bg-[#0A0A18] border border-white/5 hover:border-[#007AFF]/50 transition-colors p-6 flex flex-col items-start gap-4 h-full relative overflow-hidden group

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5 or 2, color matching parent text or accent

interactions.hover_effects: Slight lift (-translate-y-1), border glow, text color shift to primary
interactions.marquee: Slow scrolling text for 'BUY | SELL | FIX' to create dynamic urban feel

accessibility.contrast_check: Ensure all text on dark backgrounds is at least WCAG AA compliant. Use text-shadow if placing text on images.
accessibility.focus_states: Visible focus ring with color #007AFF offset-2

general: Implement the DARK MODE theme by default. Override tailwind config to use the specified dark palette.
Use 'Barlow Condensed' for all headings. Import it from Google Fonts.
Create a 'Bento Grid' layout for the Services section on the homepage.
The 'Contact' section must include the Google Map embed and the store address explicitly.
The 'Hero' section needs to be high impact - use the store storefront or gallery collage as a background with a dark overlay.
file_structure: Create modular components in /src/components/ (e.g., ServiceCard.js, Hero.js, ContactForm.js).
Keep pages in /src/pages/ (Home.js, Products.js, Policies.js, Contact.js).
specific_fixes: Ensure the 'Contact Form' just shows a success message (e.g., toast or inline text) and does not try to hit a real backend endpoint.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
